markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar’s repeated delays have pushed Grand Theft Auto VI to a November 19, 2026 target after earlier 2025 and spring 2026 windows slipped, extending the window for unrelated events to resolve first. Traders are pricing in near-certainty around a Russia-Ukraine ceasefire alongside roughly even odds on a GPT-6 launch, new Rihanna or Playboi Carti albums, and shifts in U.S. politics. Upcoming Take-Two earnings calls and any surprise Rockstar marketing drops could sharpen focus on whether the game’s timeline moves again, while chart performance, streaming milestones, and major tech announcements remain the clearest swing factors in this wide-open market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games' repeated official delays have cemented trader confidence that Grand Theft Auto VI will miss any release window before June 2026. After shifting the title from fall 2025 to May 26, 2026, and then to November 19, 2026, the studio cited the need for additional polish on its sprawling open-world experience set in Vice City. Recent earnings calls and statements from Take-Two Interactive leadership have reaffirmed the November date with no signs of acceleration, aligning with Rockstar's historical pattern of prioritizing quality over rushed launches. While an unexpected early drop remains theoretically possible if development miraculously accelerates, the barriers of certification, marketing ramp-up, and platform approvals make such a scenario highly improbable.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The recent mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s third New York rape retrial on May 15, 2026, has reinforced trader consensus around “No Prison Time” at 75.4% implied probability, as prosecutors weigh whether to pursue a fourth attempt amid repeated hung juries and evidentiary challenges. Weinstein already serves a 16-year California sentence from 2022 that remains under appeal, while his original 23-year New York conviction was overturned in 2024 on fairness grounds. Ongoing legal uncertainty, including possible plea discussions or successful appeals, limits expectations for substantial additional time, with the next-closest outcomes (20-30 years at 8.5%) reflecting only modest bets on consecutive sentencing. Upcoming decisions on retrial or appeal outcomes could shift momentum in this closely watched #MeToo case.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2026 Stanley Cup playoffs have narrowed to the conference finals, where the Carolina Hurricanes and Vegas Golden Knights hold the strongest positions as title contenders. The Hurricanes enter their Eastern Conference Final against the Montreal Canadiens tied 1-1 after splitting the first two games, building on a dominant regular season and deep playoff run that reflects sustained depth and defensive structure. In the West, the Golden Knights have seized a commanding 3-0 series lead over the Colorado Avalanche through resilient comebacks and elite goaltending, positioning them for a potential sweep of the Presidents’ Trophy winners. The Canadiens’ unexpected advancement as an underdog adds variance to the East, while the Avalanche’s steep series deficit has sharply reduced their championship odds. Trader consensus prices these outcomes accordingly, with recent series results driving the latest shifts in implied probabilities.

In the West, the Golden Knights have seized a commanding 3-0 series lead over the Colorado Avalanche through resilient comebacks and elite goaltending, positioning them for a potential sweep of the Presidents’ Trophy winners. The Canadiens’ unexpected advancement as an underdog adds variance to the East, while the Avalanche’s steep series deficit has sharply reduced their championship odds. Trader consensus prices these outcomes accordingly, with recent series results driving the latest shifts in implied probabilities.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping maintains firm control over the Chinese Communist Party as General Secretary and Central Military Commission Chairman, with ongoing anti-corruption campaigns and military leadership adjustments reinforcing centralized authority rather than signaling transition. No designated successor has emerged, and elite politics show limited visible opposition ahead of the 21st Party Congress scheduled for late 2027. Recent official messaging, including Xi’s New Year address outlining continuity through the 15th Five-Year Plan period beginning in 2026, aligns with trader expectations of stability through the end of the year. While health rumors and personnel shifts occasionally surface in analysis, they lack confirmation of imminent removal and have not shifted the 93% implied probability against such an outcome before 2027. Late-breaking events like sudden institutional challenges remain the primary variables that could still alter this consensus.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2028 Democratic presidential nomination market shows a fragmented field, with Gavin Newsom at 24.3% leading a broad group of contenders including Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez at 9.0% and Kamala Harris at 8.5%. No candidate has consolidated majority support, reflecting diverse preferences across party factions, regional bases, and ideological priorities. Differentiators among leaders include prior executive or legislative experience, national visibility, fundraising capacity, and appeal to key voter coalitions such as progressives, moderates, and swing-state demographics. Scheduled events like midterm contests, primary debates, and early-state polling could shift probabilities by clarifying paths to delegate majorities under party rules.
